marketBlend.conclusion: CONFIRMED: modelWeight≈0.15 shrinks |blended−posted| to ~15% of independent gap. Star playability (key-number differential) then selects a thin tail — not a valid apples-to-apples comparison to v2/v3-independent without a different playability criterion.

spreadToWinProb.note: Same mapping as v2 (k so +3 → ~0.60). Hand check: +3 should be ~0.60.

brier.overconfidence: v3 playable |modelSpread| mean 10.43 vs v2 5.22 — same logistic maps larger spreads to more extreme probs, inflating Brier when wrong.
brier.clipExperiment: Clipping v3 spreads to ±7 improves Brier 0.2457 → 0.2304 (diagnostic only, not a fix).

qbVolatility.sampleCaveat: qb-ratings-v3-sample.json only stores week 1 & 10 snapshots — per-week rates are interpolated across that gap.
qbVolatility.note: QB point deltas are not dramatically larger than team wow on this sample.

wepa.productionNote: calibrate-v3 currently feeds weekly player EPA sums into team updates — play-level weightPlay() is unit-tested but not on the live rating path.

overall: Primary Brier driver: overconfident |modelSpread| under the shared spread→P map (not a new conversion). Market-blend instability: differential shrinkage under star filter. WEPA weightPlay not on production path — cannot explain current Brier. Architecture needs more than coefficient retuning before claiming v3 > v2.
